title: Nephrodium kilemense
taxonomy: Nephrodium kilemense is classified as a species of fern in the family Dryopteridaceae. It is an extinct genus of ferns, which belonged to the order Polypodiales. The classification of Nephrodium kilemense is further refined to the family level, where it is placed alongside other dryopteridaceous ferns. Nephrodium kilemense is also known to be a part of the division Pteridophyta, which comprises all ferns and fern allies. This classification helps in understanding the evolutionary relationships and placing Nephrodium kilemense within the broader context of plant taxonomy.
uses: Nephrodium kilemense is a species of fern that has been used in traditional medicine in various parts of the world. The medicinal properties of this plant are primarily attributed to its antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antibacterial compounds. Traditionally, it has been used to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive problems. However, more research is needed to fully understand the efficacy and potential side effects of using Nephrodium kilemense as a medicinal plant. Further studies are required to validate its medicinal uses and ensure safe consumption.

constituents: Nephrodium kilemense, a fern species, has been studied for its potential medicinal properties, although detailed information on its active constituents is limited. parts: Nephrodium kilemense, a species of fern, has been traditionally used for medicinal purposes in various parts of the world. preparations: Nephrodium kilemense, a plant species found in East Africa, has been traditionally used by local communities to prepare herbal remedies for various health conditions.
